solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int integerReplacement(int n) {
        int count = 0;
        while (n != 1) {
            if (n % 2 == 0) {
                n /= 2;
            } else {
                if ((n - 1) % 4 == 0 && n != 3) {
                    n++;
                } else {
                    n--;
                }
            }
            count++;
        }
        return count;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The algorithm uses a greedy approach to minimize the number of operations by making optimal choices for odd numbers based on modulo 4 patterns. It avoids unnecessary increments when possible, leading to efficient execution.>
